project_name: MySQL to BigQuery Data Ingestion Pipeline
objective: Design and implement a reliable and scalable pipeline to load MySQL tables (agentichub database, employees table) into Google BigQuery (verizon_data_deah dataset) on a scheduled basis, supporting both a one-time full historical load and ongoing scheduled incremental loads (hourly or daily per table) to enable analytics, reporting, and long-term data storage.
business_context: This initiative enables analytics and reporting teams to access MySQL source data reliably within BigQuery at scale. By migrating and continuously syncing data, the organization achieves long-term data storage, 100% historical completeness, and self-service analytics capabilities. Stakeholder Yash requires independent visibility into load health without developer involvement, making operational transparency a first-class requirement alongside data correctness.
functional_requirements: Execute a one-time full historical load of the MySQL employees table (agentichub) into BigQuery verizon_data_deah dataset with 100% data completeness and no data loss
Implement a scheduled incremental data load pipeline that continuously syncs new and updated records from MySQL employees table to BigQuery on a per-table cadence (hourly or daily)
Track incremental changes using timestamp-based or primary key delta strategies, consistently applied across all runs
Perform pre-load schema compatibility checks covering data types, null handling, field names, and new column handling strategy before each load execution
Perform post-load row count validation between MySQL source and BigQuery target for every load cycle
Enforce primary key uniqueness in BigQuery after every load execution
Define and implement a predefined handling strategy for new columns appearing in MySQL before they are loaded into BigQuery
Generate and store audit logs for every load execution, accessible after each run
Implement a monitoring dashboard or log-based visibility layer enabling stakeholders to independently review load success without developer involvement
Trigger monitoring alerts on pipeline failures within a defined SLA
Gate incremental load activation: incremental pipeline must not begin until historical load validation is fully passed and signed off
Log and report null constraint violations before marking any load as complete
Prevent data duplication and gaps across all incremental load cycles

source_systems: MySQL — agentichub database, employees table, hosted on GCP Cloud SQL instance verizon-data:us-central1:mysql-druid-metadatastore
data_requirements.frequency: Daily (primary schedule interval); hourly or daily per table as defined per individual table requirements

non_functional.performance: Full historical load must complete without data loss; row counts between MySQL and BigQuery must match 100% for all historical and incremental loads
non_functional.scalability: Pipeline must be reliable and scalable to support ongoing scheduled loads, potential additional tables, and both hourly and daily cadences
non_functional.latency: New and updated MySQL records must appear in BigQuery within the defined refresh interval (hourly or daily per table)
non_functional.sla: Monitoring alerts must trigger on pipeline failures within a defined SLA; specific SLA duration not quantified in source

inferred_assumptions: Technology stack likely requires a GCP-native orchestration or ETL framework (e.g., Cloud Composer, Dataflow, or Cloud Run Jobs) as no specific pipeline framework or tool is named in the requirements
Deployment environment is assumed to be production or near-production given stakeholder review status and analytics team dependency, but no environment tier is explicitly stated
Data types are assumed to be standard MySQL column types requiring explicit mapping to BigQuery-compatible types; no column-level type list is provided in the source
acceptance_criteria: Full historical load completes without data loss for all identified tables
Scheduled incremental load jobs execute on the defined hourly or daily cadence without manual intervention or failures
Row counts between MySQL and BigQuery match for all historical and incremental loads
Analytics teams confirm data is reliable and usable for reporting
Incremental loads do not activate until historical load validation is fully passed and signed off by stakeholders
Data types, null handling, and field names are validated before each load execution
Primary key uniqueness is maintained in BigQuery after every load
New columns appearing in MySQL follow a predefined handling strategy before being loaded
Audit logs are consistently written and accessible after each load execution
Schema validation catches and logs mismatches before data reaches BigQuery
Monitoring alerts trigger on pipeline failures within a defined SLA
Stakeholders can access load status independently without requiring developer involvement
Null constraint violations are logged and reported before a load is marked complete
No data duplication or gaps are detected across any incremental load cycle
expected_outputs: All agreed-upon MySQL tables present in BigQuery (verizon_data_deah dataset) with 100% historical completeness
Automated scheduled incremental load pipeline syncing the MySQL employees table to BigQuery on an hourly or daily cadence
Pre-load schema compatibility validation reports covering data types, null handling, field names, and new column strategy
Post-load row count and primary key uniqueness validation reports for every load cycle
Audit log records generated and accessible for every load execution
Monitoring dashboard or log-based visibility layer enabling self-service stakeholder review of load health and status
Documented and implemented new column handling strategy
low_confidence_warning: No orchestration framework or ETL tooling is specified (preferred_tools is empty), leaving the implementation stack partially undefined. The SLA duration for monitoring alert triggers is referenced but not quantified. No data volume, data sensitivity, or environment tier information is provided.
raw_text:
Ticket: SCRUM-123
Summary: Build MySQL to BigQuery Data Ingestion Pipeline
Status: To Do
Priority: Medium
Assignee: Unassigned
Description:
Project Name: MySQL to BigQuery Data Ingestion Pipeline Requirement Type: New_Dvlp Stakeholder: Yash Schedule Interval: daily Objective Design and implement a reliable and scalable pipeline to load MySQL tables into BigQuery on a scheduled basis to support analytics, reporting, and long-term data storage. Design and implement a reliable, scalable pipeline to load tables from the MySQL database (agentichub, employees table) into Google BigQuery (verizon_data_deah dataset). The pipeline must support both a one-time full historical load and ongoing scheduled incremental loads (hourly or daily per table). This initiative supports analytics, reporting, and long-term data storage. Status: Draft — Pending Stakeholder Review. Expected Outcome All agreed-upon tables are present in BigQuery with 100% historical completeness, scheduled data refreshes run reliably, and analytics teams can confidently use the data. Connections and DB Details Source: Connection verizon-data:us-central1:mysql-druid-metadatastore, IP 34.70.79.163, Port 3306, DB agentichub, Table employees, Username sa. Target: BigQuery GCP Project verizon-data, Dataset verizon_data_deah, Table employees. Acceptance Criteria Success Conditions: Full historical load completes without data loss for all identified tables Scheduled incremental load jobs run hourly or daily without failures Row counts between MySQL and BigQuery match for all historical loads Analytics teams confirm data is reliable and usable for reporting Validation Rules: Incremental loads must not begin until historical load validation has fully passed Data types, null handling, and field names must be validated before each load Primary key uniqueness must be maintained in BigQuery after every load New columns appearing in MySQL must follow a defined handling strategy Audit logs must be maintained for every load execution

--- Related Tickets (2) ---

Related Ticket: SCRUM-124
Summary: Implement scheduled incremental load pipeline from MySQL to BigQuery
Status: To Do
Priority: Medium
Description:
Project Name: MySQL to BigQuery Data Ingestion Pipeline Requirement Type: New_Dvlp Stakeholder: Yash Schedule Interval: daily Objective Keep BigQuery tables continuously up-to-date with MySQL source data after the historical load to support reliable, timely analytics and reporting. Design and implement a scheduled incremental data load pipeline that continuously syncs new and updated records from the MySQL employees table (agentichub database, connection verizon-data:us-central1:mysql-druid-metadatastore) into BigQuery dataset verizon_data_deah after the full historical load is validated. Tracking must be based on timestamp or primary key delta strategies. Per-table schedule to run hourly or daily as defined per table requirements. Expected Outcome Incremental loads run automatically on schedule with new MySQL data appearing in BigQuery within the defined refresh interval (hourly or daily per table), with no data gaps or duplicates. Connections and DB Details Source: verizon-data:us-central1:mysql-druid-metadatastore, IP 34.70.79.163, Port 3306, DB agentichub, Table employees, Username sa. Target: BigQuery GCP Project verizon-data, Dataset verizon_data_deah, Table employees. Acceptance Criteria Success Conditions: Scheduled jobs execute on the defined hourly or daily cadence without manual intervention Incremental records in MySQL are reflected accurately in BigQuery within the schedule window No data duplication or gaps detected across incremental load cycles Validation Rules: Incremental pipeline must not activate before historical load validation is signed off Delta tracking mechanism (timestamp or primary key) must be consistent and reliable across runs Primary key uniqueness must be maintained in BigQuery after each incremental load

Related Ticket: SCRUM-125
Summary: Set up pipeline monitoring, schema validation, and audit logging
Status: To Do
Priority: Medium
Description:
Project Name: MySQL to BigQuery Data Ingestion Pipeline Requirement Type: New_Dvlp Stakeholder: Yash Schedule Interval: on-demand Objective Ensure data quality, schema consistency, and operational visibility for the MySQL to BigQuery pipeline, enabling early detection of issues and giving stakeholders independent confidence in the data. Implement end-to-end data quality controls, schema validation, monitoring, alerting, and audit logging for the MySQL to BigQuery ingestion pipeline. Covers: pre-load schema compatibility checks (data types, null handling, field names, new column handling strategy); post-load row count and primary key uniqueness validation between MySQL and BigQuery; audit log generation for every load execution; and a monitoring dashboard or log-based visibility layer enabling stakeholders (Yash) to independently review load success without developer involvement. Expected Outcome All loads are validated and fully audited; schema mismatches are caught before they impact reporting; stakeholders can independently verify load health via a dashboard or log view. Connections and DB Details Source: verizon-data:us-central1:mysql-druid-metadatastore, IP 34.70.79.163, Port 3306, DB agentichub, Table employees, Username sa. Target: BigQuery GCP Project verizon-data, Dataset verizon_data_deah, Table employees. Acceptance Criteria Success Conditions: Schema validation catches and logs mismatches before data reaches BigQuery Row count and primary key validation pass for every load cycle Audit logs are consistently written and accessible after each load execution Monitoring alerts trigger on pipeline failures within a defined SLA Stakeholders can access load status independently without requiring developer involvement Validation Rules: Data types, null handling, and field names must be validated before each load execution New columns in MySQL must follow a predefined handling strategy before being loaded Primary key uniqueness must be enforced in BigQuery after every load Null constraint violations must be logged and reported before the load is marked complete
